text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet valley surrounded by towering forests, there lived a shy squirrel named Thumper. Thumper didnít often talk; instead, he lived happily gathering nuts through the forest's ancient oak trees. One day, while filling his pouch with walnuts, Thumper heard a soft rustling behind a big ol' pine.

Curious, Thumper moved cautiously toward the noise. It turned out to be a tiny owl, owl eyesight impeccable despite its bobbing motion. The owl greeted Thumper warmly and suggested they become friends. Thumper wasnít a talker, but he appreciated owl wisdom about the land.

Over the weeks, owl and squirrel became great friends. One chilly evening, when the moon was full, owl fluttered down beside Thumper. "You know," owl said softly, "in the nights without stars, I often dream of forests full of birds just like you."

Thumper blushed slightly. "Really? Thatís... special?"
